clock_offset_readout_query: Use the displayed time, apply the requested minute offset, then choose the matching answer option.
Read the analog clock as the starting time, apply the minute offset, and select the correct option card.
Start from the shown clock time and use the answer cards below the clock.
Base the calculation on the clock face and choose the option card with the resulting time.
Use the analog clock as the starting time; the answer is one of the option labels below.
clock_elapsed_time_value_query: Use clocks A and B, then choose the answer card showing the forward elapsed minutes.
Read both labeled clocks and select the option card with the elapsed time in minutes.
Compare clock A to clock B moving forward, using the answer cards below.
Find the forward elapsed time from A to B and choose its option label.
Use the two clock faces and the six answer cards below them.
clock_time_order_label_query: Order the labeled clocks by the times shown. Within the 12-hour cycle, 12 o'clock comes before 1 o'clock.
Compare the four labeled clock times. Treat the 12-hour cycle as starting at 12, then 1, then later hours.
Use the labeled analog clocks and the answer cards. The ordering is earliest to latest in a single 12-hour cycle starting at 12.
Read each labeled clock, then choose the card whose label order runs from earliest to latest, with 12 before 1.
Sort the four clock labels by shown time in the same 12-hour cycle, where 12 is the start of the cycle.
clock_hand_angle_value_query: Use the two hands on the analog clock and choose the matching degree option.
Compare the hour and minute hands, then select the option card with the smaller angle.
Use the hour hand and minute hand shown on the clock; answer with an option label.
Base the answer on the smaller angle formed by the two hands and choose the matching card.
Read the hand positions on the clock face and use the answer options below.
clock_full_time_readout_query: Use all three hands on the analog clock and choose the matching HH:MM:SS option.
Read the hour, minute, and thin second hand, then select the correct option card.
Use the three visible hand positions and the six time options below the clock.
Base the time on the hour hand, minute hand, and thin second hand; answer with an option label.
Read the complete analog clock display and choose the matching option below.
clock_alarm_wait_time_value_query: The dark hour and minute hands show the current time. The red alarm hand marks an alarm hour with alarm minutes fixed at :00. Choose the option with the forward wait time in minutes.
Read the current time from the dark hands. The red alarm hand uses the clock's 1-12 hour scale with alarm minutes fixed at :00; select the matching minute option.
Use the dark hands for the current time and the red hour-scale alarm pointer for the alarm time, then choose the wait-time option.
The black/blue hands show the current time. The red alarm hand points at the alarm hour with alarm minutes fixed at :00, and the answer is one of the minute options below.
Treat the red alarm hand as a distinct hour-scale alarm pointer with alarm minutes fixed at :00, and choose the option card with the forward wait time.

minutes_after: Which option shows the time {delta_minutes} minutes after the shown time?
Starting from the displayed time, choose the option for {delta_minutes} minutes later.
Shift the shown time forward by {delta_minutes} minutes. Which option matches?
Use a {delta_minutes}-minute offset after the displayed time. Which option is correct?
Add {delta_minutes} minutes to the shown time and choose the matching option.
minutes_before: Which option shows the time {delta_minutes} minutes before the shown time?
Starting from the displayed time, choose the option for {delta_minutes} minutes earlier.
Shift the shown time backward by {delta_minutes} minutes. Which option matches?
Use a {delta_minutes}-minute offset before the displayed time. Which option is correct?
Subtract {delta_minutes} minutes from the shown time and choose the matching option.
earliest_time_label: Which labeled clock shows the earliest time?
Identify the labeled clock with the earliest shown time.
Among the labeled clocks, which one shows the earliest time?
Find the clock showing the earliest time.
Which labeled clock shows the earliest time among the clocks shown?
latest_time_label: Which labeled clock shows the latest time?
Identify the labeled clock with the latest shown time.
Among the labeled clocks, which one shows the latest time?
Find the clock showing the latest time.
Which labeled clock shows the latest time among the clocks shown?
analog_reference_digital_options: Which labeled digital display shows the same time as the reference clock?
Choose the digital-display option that matches the reference clock time.
Which labeled digital option is equivalent to the reference analog clock?
Find the labeled digital display with the same time as the reference clock.
Which digital option matches the time shown by the reference clock?
digital_reference_analog_options: Which labeled analog clock shows the same time as the reference display?
Choose the analog-clock option that matches the reference digital time.
Which labeled analog option is equivalent to the reference digital display?
Find the labeled analog clock with the same time as the reference display.
Which analog option matches the time shown by the reference display?
elapsed_time_value: Moving forward around the clock, which option gives how many minutes later clock B is than clock A?
Which option gives the minutes elapsed from clock A to clock B when time moves forward?
Starting at clock A and moving forward, choose the option for the minutes before the time shown by clock B.
What option shows the forward elapsed time in minutes from clock A to clock B?
Count the minutes forward from clock A to clock B. Which option is correct?
sequence_completion_label: Which option completes the time pattern in the empty box?
Choose the option clock that belongs in the missing sequence slot.
Which labeled option should fill the empty box in the clock sequence?
Find the option that completes the four-box time sequence.
Which option continues the time pattern for the blank box?
time_order_label: Which option lists the clock labels from earliest to latest?
Choose the option card that orders the clocks from earliest to latest.
Which numbered option shows the correct earliest-to-latest order?
Select the option whose label sequence is sorted by the shown clock times.
Which answer card gives the four clock labels in time order from earliest to latest?
hand_angle_value: Which option gives the smaller angle in degrees between the hour hand and the minute hand?
Find the smaller angle formed by the two clock hands, then choose the matching option.
Which option shows how many degrees separate the hour hand and minute hand along the shorter arc?
Measure the smaller angle between the clock hands. Which option is correct?
What is the smaller hand-to-hand angle on the clock? Choose the matching option.
full_time_readout: Which option gives the exact time shown in HH:MM:SS format?
Read the clock including seconds. Which option matches it?
Which option gives the complete time shown by the three hands?
Choose the option with the exact displayed time in HH:MM:SS format.
What time do the hour, minute, and thin second hands show? Choose the matching option.
alarm_wait_time_value: Moving forward from the current time, which option gives the minutes remaining until the next alarm time?
Which option gives how many minutes are left before the next occurrence of the alarm time?
Count forward from the shown current time to the red-hand alarm time. Which option is correct?
What option shows the forward wait time, in minutes, until the alarm time?
Starting at the current time shown by the dark hands, choose the option for minutes until the next alarm.

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_offset_readout_classi: one analog clock with all hour numerals, minute ticks, and two visible hands with six labeled time answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_offset_readout_minima: one clean analog clock with hour numerals and two visible hands with six labeled time answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_offset_readout_outlin: one outline-style analog clock with hour numerals and two visible hands with six labeled time answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_extremum_label_c: six labeled analog clocks arranged in a centered grid, each with hour numerals, minute ticks, and two visible hands
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_extremum_label_m: six clean labeled analog clocks arranged in a centered grid, each with hour numerals and two visible hands
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_extremum_label_o: six labeled outline-style analog clocks arranged in a centered grid, each with hour numerals and two visible hands
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_equivalent_time_label: one analog reference clock above six labeled digital-display options
one digital reference display above six labeled analog-clock options
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_elapsed_time_value_cl: two labeled analog clocks, A and B, shown side by side with hour numerals, minute ticks, and two visible hands and six labeled minute answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_elapsed_time_value_mi: two clean labeled analog clocks, A and B, shown side by side with hour numerals and two visible hands and six labeled minute answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_elapsed_time_value_ou: two labeled outline-style analog clocks, A and B, shown side by side with hour numerals and two visible hands and six labeled minute answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_sequence_completion_l: a top row of four clock boxes with one empty box, and a bottom row of four labeled option clocks
a clean top row of four clock boxes with one empty box, and a bottom row of four labeled option clocks
an outline-style top row of four clock boxes with one empty box, and a bottom row of four labeled option clocks
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_order_label_clas: four labeled analog clocks in a top row and six numbered answer cards below, each card listing a candidate order of the clock labels
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_order_label_mini: four clean labeled analog clocks in a top row and six numbered answer cards below, each card listing a candidate order of the clock labels
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_time_order_label_outl: four labeled outline-style analog clocks in a top row and six numbered answer cards below, each card listing a candidate order of the clock labels
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_hand_angle_value_clas: one analog clock with all hour numerals, minute ticks, and two visible hands with six labeled degree answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_hand_angle_value_mini: one clean analog clock with hour numerals and two visible hands with six labeled degree answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_hand_angle_value_outl: one outline-style analog clock with hour numerals and two visible hands with six labeled degree answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_full_time_readout_cla: one analog clock with all hour numerals, minute ticks, and three visible hands with six labeled HH:MM:SS answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_full_time_readout_min: one clean analog clock with hour numerals and three visible hands with six labeled HH:MM:SS answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_full_time_readout_out: one outline-style analog clock with hour numerals and three visible hands with six labeled HH:MM:SS answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_alarm_wait_time_value: one analog clock with all hour numerals, minute ticks, two dark current-time hands, and one red alarm hand with six labeled minute answer cards below
one clean analog clock with hour numerals, two dark current-time hands, and one red alarm hand with six labeled minute answer cards below
one outline-style analog clock with hour numerals, two dark current-time hands, and one red alarm hand with six labeled minute answer cards below
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_offset_readout: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected answer option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_offset_readout: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_time_extremum_label: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected clock face
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_time_extremum_label: set "answer" to the single selected clock label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_equivalent_time_label: set "annotation" to an object/dictionary with keys "reference" and "correct_option", each containing a visual bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_equivalent_time_label: set "answer" to the single matching option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_elapsed_time_value: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected answer option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_elapsed_time_value: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_sequence_completion_labe: set "annotation" to an object/dictionary with keys "sequence_panel" and "correct_option", each containing a bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_sequence_completion_label: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_time_order_label: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the correct answer card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_time_order_label: set "answer" to the selected numbered option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_hand_angle_value: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected answer option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_hand_angle_value: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_full_time_readout: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected answer option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_full_time_readout: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_alarm_wait_time_value: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the selected answer option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_alarm_wait_time_value: set "answer" to the single selected option label as a string
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract: Use a valid JSON object with keys "annotation" and "answer" in that order for the final answer.
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract_answer_only: Use a valid JSON object with key "answer" for the final answer.
